steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Recall the volume formula for a cube
steps.content: For a cube with edge length $s$, the volume equals the product of its three equal dimensions: length times width times height, all equal to $s$.

steps.title: Substitute and calculate
steps.content:
With edge length $s = 4$ inches:
$$V = 4^3 = 4 \times 4 \times 4 = 64 \ \text{cubic inches}$$
The volume is 64 cubic inches.
